title: ggposter: build an A1 conference poster from ggplot2
text: ggposter builds poster declaratively: theme controls colours, fonts spacing; title band spans full width; rest poster set columns, vertical stack cards. card rounded box accent-coloured header tab one piece content – text, table, ggplot figure, strip photos. Every card built plain [grid] grob, whole poster assembled one [gtable::gtable], building blocks work whether content bullet list ggplot. Content layout described either R list, YAML read [read_poster_yaml()]. Figures, tables, photos referenced YAML passed separately R objects (objects = list(...)), YAML stays purely declarative data analysis stays R.
code: library(ggposter) library(ggplot2)

title: ggposter: build an A1 conference poster from ggplot2
text: p ggposter object – thin wrapper around assembled gtable. Font sizes spacing absolute physical measurements sized poster’s true paper size, previewing arbitrary plot size (vignette must) needs scaled-render rather printing p directly:  Save true size [render_poster()]:
code: spec <- list(   title = list(title = "A minimal ggposter example"),   layout = list(left = "intro", right = "figure"),   sections = list(     intro = list(       header = "INTRODUCTION",       body = list(type = "text", md = c(         "- Poster content is declared as an R list or YAML.",         "- Figures and tables are passed in as R objects.",         "- The whole poster renders as one gtable object."       ))     ),     figure = list(       header = "A FIGURE",       body = list(type = "figure", object = "fig")     )   ) )  fig <- ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, colour = drv)) +   geom_point() +   theme_bw()  p <- poster(spec, objects = list(fig = fig)) render_poster(p, "ggposter-minimal-preview.png", scale = 0.25, dpi = 150) knitr::include_graphics("ggposter-minimal-preview.png") render_poster(p, "poster.pdf")          # true A1 size, fonts embedded render_poster(p, "preview.png", scale = 0.25, dpi = 150)  # A4-ish preview

title: ggposter: build an A1 conference poster from ggplot2
text: Every section header (NULL header) body type: "text" – character vector markdown-ish lines, rendered via [card_text()]. Lines starting "- " become bulleted. "table" – data.frame/tibble named objects, rendered via [card_table()] booktab-style table optional title/caption. table’s font size shrunk automatically fit column. "figure" – ggplot object named objects, rendered via [card_figure()], re-themed poster’s base font size/family. "image" – one photo files, rendered via [card_image()] labelled row (e.g. “dominant species” photo band).

title: ggposter: build an A1 conference poster from ggplot2
text: [poster_theme()] controls accent colour, fonts, corner radius, spacing; [theme_green()] ready-made preset. Latin CJK (Japanese) fonts set independently, [poster_font()] picks installed family automatically PDF export embeds real glyphs rather falling back substitute:
code: th <- theme_green(base_size = 24, base_family = poster_font()) th #>  #> ── poster_theme ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> accent: #2E7D32 #> box_fill: #FFFFFF #> box_border: #2E7D32 #> base_family: DejaVu Sans #> cjk_family: DejaVu Sans #> base_size: 24 pt # For a Japanese poster, pick an installed CJK-capable family: th_jp <- poster_theme(base_family = poster_font(cjk = TRUE))

title: ggposter: build an A1 conference poster from ggplot2
text: following reproduces layout text real conference poster (poster.pdf package’s development repository): full-width title band two-column body rounded, tab-headed cards introduction/background/methods left conclusions/results right. title, author list, funding note, body text tables transcribed verbatim original poster. cutting-regime scatterplot community ordination plot illustrative, generated reported per-community means standard deviations (original point-level field data, published poster). photos generic stock images bundled package, standing original species photographs. layout, theme, section text live YAML file bundled package: Assembling rendering:
code: tbl_courses <- tibble::tribble(   ~`Golf course`, ~`Established year`, ~`Area (ha)`, ~`Total grassland spp.`,   ~`Grassland spp. (mean +/- SD /m2)`, ~`All spp. (mean +/- SD /m2)`,   "A", 1903, 20, 79, "6.5 +/- 2.8", "11.2 +/- 4.6",   "B", 1926, 70, 55, "2.9 +/- 2.0", "5.2 +/- 4.6",   "C", 1930, 55, 78, "4.4 +/- 2.8", "8.1 +/- 5.1",   "D", 1956, 60, 55, "2.1 +/- 1.5", "4.0 +/- 3.3" )  tbl_community <- tibble::tribble(   ~Community, ~`No. of plots`, ~`Grassland spp. (/m2)`, ~`All spp. (/m2)`,   ~`Cutting height (cm)`, ~`Cutting freq. (/yr)`,   "com1", 57, "6.1 +/- 2.9", "10.7 +/- 4.7", "12.9 +/- 15.3", "4.2 +/- 6.1",   "com2", 38, "5.3 +/- 2.7", "10.6 +/- 5.2", "22.7 +/- 19.2", "4.4 +/- 4.4",   "com3", 37, "3.9 +/- 1.3", "6.9 +/- 3.1",  "5.1 +/- 4.8",   "10.1 +/- 5.3",   "com4", 68, "1.4 +/- 0.7", "2.4 +/- 1.6",  "5.2 +/- 5.0",   "14.6 +/- 5.5" ) set.seed(42) community_params <- tibble::tribble(   ~Community, ~n, ~height_mean, ~height_sd, ~freq_mean, ~freq_sd,   "com1", 57, 12.9, 15.3, 4.2, 6.1,   "com2", 38, 22.7, 19.2, 4.4, 4.4,   "com3", 37, 5.1,  4.8,  10.1, 5.3,   "com4", 68, 5.2,  5.0,  14.6, 5.5 )  # illustrative points matching the reported mean +/- SD, not raw field data df_cutting <- purrr::list_rbind(purrr::pmap(community_params, function(Community, n,                                                           height_mean, height_sd,                                                           freq_mean, freq_sd) {   tibble::tibble(     Community = Community,     cutting_height = pmax(0, rnorm(n, height_mean, height_sd)),     cutting_freq   = pmax(1, rnorm(n, freq_mean, freq_sd))   ) })) fig_cutting <- ggplot(df_cutting, aes(cutting_freq, cutting_height)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.6, colour = "#2E7D32") +   facet_wrap(~Community, nrow = 1) +   labs(x = "Cutting frequency (/yr)", y = "Cutting height (cm)") +   theme_bw()  df_ord <- purrr::list_rbind(purrr::pmap(community_params, function(Community, n, ...) {   centre <- switch(Community,     com1 = c(1, 1), com2 = c(1, -1), com3 = c(-1, 0.5), com4 = c(-1.2, -1))   tibble::tibble(Community = Community,                  NMDS1 = rnorm(n, centre[1], 0.4),                  NMDS2 = rnorm(n, centre[2], 0.4)) })) fig_ordination <- ggplot(df_ord, aes(NMDS1, NMDS2, colour = Community)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +   stat_ellipse() +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") yml_path <- system.file("extdata", "poster_sample.yml", package = "ggposter") cat(readLines(yml_path)[1:20], sep = "\n") poster:   size: A1   orientation: portrait  theme:   accent: "#2E7D32"   base_size: 24  title:   title: "The relationship between grassland species richness and the management in long-established golf courses in Japan"   authors: "*Toshikazu Matsumura (Konan Women's Univ.), Yoshinobu Hashimoto (Museum of Nature and Human activities, Hyogo), Yoshihiro Sawada (Awaji Landscape Planning & Horticulture Academy / Univ. of Hyogo)"   funding: "This study was supported by JSPS KAKENHI Grant Number 15K07833. We wish to thank the golf courses for field investigations."  layout:   left:  [objectives, backgrounds, methods, course_summary, dominant_species]   right: [conclusions, community_table, community_figure, ordination, other_species]  sections:   objectives:     header: "OBJECTIVES" sample_poster <- poster(   yml_path,   objects = list(     tbl_courses = tbl_courses, tbl_community = tbl_community,     fig_cutting = fig_cutting, fig_ordination = fig_ordination   ),   theme = theme_green(base_size = 24),   base_dir = dirname(yml_path) ) render_poster(sample_poster, "ggposter-sample-preview.png", scale = 0.3, dpi = 150) knitr::include_graphics("ggposter-sample-preview.png") render_poster(sample_poster, "sample_poster.pdf")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Toshikazu Matsumura. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Matsumura T (2026). ggposter: Build A1 Conference Posters ggplot2 YAML. R package version 0.6.0, https://matutosi.github.io/ggposter/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ggposter: Build A1 Conference Posters from ggplot2 with YAML},   author = {Toshikazu Matsumura},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.6.0},   url = {https://matutosi.github.io/ggposter/}, }

what: Build A1 Conference Posters from ggplot2 with YAML
title: Build A1 Conference Posters from ggplot2 with YAML
text: ggposter builds A1 (true-size) conference poster ggplot2. poster declared title band plus columns rounded, tab-headed cards – text, tables, ggplot2 figures, photo strips – assembled grid gtable rendered true size embedded fonts, including CJK (Japanese). Content layout can written R list YAML file; figures, tables, photos supplied separately R objects.

title: Build A1 Conference Posters from ggplot2 with YAML
text: can install development version ggposter GitHub :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("matutosi/ggposter")

title: Build A1 Conference Posters from ggplot2 with YAML
text: layout follows typical conference poster – full-width title band two-column body rounded, tab-headed cards introduction/methods/summary left results/conclusions right – filled mpg fuel-economy dataset bundled ggplot2. title, author, affiliation placeholders. also shows two features keeping card’s height tied ’s actually , instead fixed proportion column: height = "auto" sizes card fit content, notes puts bullet-list description beside table figure. Rendering poster true size makes font sizes spacing come correctly proportioned; previewing arbitrary plot size (README ) , render scaled-preview PNG instead printing p directly:  Save true size, fonts embedded: See vignette("ggposter") full spec schema, theming, reproduction real conference poster.
code: library(ggposter) library(ggplot2)  tbl_class <- aggregate(cbind(hwy, cty) ~ class, data = mpg, FUN = function(x) round(mean(x), 1)) names(tbl_class) <- c("Class", "Mean hwy", "Mean cty") class_best  <- tbl_class$Class[which.max(tbl_class$`Mean hwy`)] class_worst <- tbl_class$Class[which.min(tbl_class$`Mean hwy`)]  tbl_drv <- aggregate(cbind(hwy, cty) ~ drv, data = mpg, FUN = function(x) round(mean(x), 1)) names(tbl_drv) <- c("Drivetrain", "Mean hwy", "Mean cty") drv_best  <- tbl_drv$Drivetrain[which.max(tbl_drv$`Mean hwy`)] drv_worst <- tbl_drv$Drivetrain[which.min(tbl_drv$`Mean hwy`)]  fig_facet <- ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy)) +   geom_point(colour = "#2E7D32") +   facet_wrap(~class, nrow = 1) +   theme_bw()  fig_scatter <- ggplot(mpg, aes(cty, hwy, colour = drv)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")  img_dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "ggposter") stock_photos <- c("small.JPG", "tall.jpg", "wide.jpg", "large.JPG") stock_labels <- c("Photo A", "Photo B", "Photo C", "Photo D")  spec <- list(   title = list(     title = "Example Poster: Fuel Economy Patterns in the mpg Dataset",     authors = "*Jane Doe (Example University), John Smith (Example Institute)",     funding = "This is a demonstration poster for the ggposter package; it does not describe real research."   ),   layout = list(     left  = c("objectives", "background", "methods", "summary_table", "photos_1"),     right = c("conclusions", "results_table", "fig_facet", "fig_scatter", "photos_2")   ),   sections = list(     objectives = list(header = "OBJECTIVES", height = "auto", body = list(type = "text", md = c(       "- Demonstrate the ggposter package.",       "- Use the mpg fuel-economy dataset as example content.",       "- Combine text, tables, figures, and photos in one poster."     ))),     background = list(header = "BACKGROUND", height = "auto", body = list(type = "text", md = c(       "- Conference posters often mix text, tables, and figures.",       "- ggposter arranges these as rounded, tab-headed cards.",       "- Layout and content can be declared as an R list or a YAML file."     ))),     methods = list(header = "METHODS", height = "auto", body = list(type = "text", md = c(       "- Data: the mpg dataset (234 vehicles, model years 1999-2008).",       "- Figures: highway/city mileage by class and drivetrain.",       "- Photos: generic stock images bundled with ggposter."     ))),     summary_table = list(header = "SUMMARY BY CLASS", height = "auto", body = list(       type = "table", object = "tbl_class", title = "Mean mileage by vehicle class",       notes = c(         sprintf("- **%s** has the best highway mileage.", class_best),         sprintf("- **%s** has the worst.", class_worst)       )     )),     photos_1 = list(header = "Sample photos", height = "auto", body = list(       type = "image", files = stock_photos, labels = stock_labels,       width = 230     )),     conclusions = list(header = "CONCLUSIONS", height = "auto", body = list(type = "text", md = c(       "- Compact and subcompact cars get the best highway mileage.",       "- SUVs and pickups get the lowest.",       "- ggposter can lay out this kind of summary automatically."     ))),     results_table = list(header = "SUMMARY BY DRIVETRAIN", height = "auto", body = list(       type = "table", object = "tbl_drv", title = "Mean mileage by drivetrain",       notes = c(         sprintf("- **%s**-wheel drive has the best highway mileage.", drv_best),         sprintf("- **%s**-wheel drive has the worst.", drv_worst)       )     )),     fig_facet = list(header = "Mileage by class", height = 1.3,       body = list(type = "figure", object = "fig_facet")),     fig_scatter = list(header = "Highway vs. city mileage", height = "auto", body = list(       type = "figure", object = "fig_scatter", notes_width = 0.45,       notes = c(         "- Highway and city mileage are closely correlated.",         "- 4-wheel drive vehicles cluster at the low-mileage end.",         "- Front-wheel drive vehicles cluster at the high-mileage end."       )     )),     photos_2 = list(header = "More sample photos", height = "auto", body = list(       type = "image", files = stock_photos, labels = stock_labels,       width = 230     ))   ) )  p <- poster(   spec,   objects = list(tbl_class = tbl_class, tbl_drv = tbl_drv,                  fig_facet = fig_facet, fig_scatter = fig_scatter),   theme = theme_green(base_size = 24),   base_dir = img_dir ) render_poster(p, "man/figures/README-poster-preview.png", scale = 0.3, dpi = 150) knitr::include_graphics("man/figures/README-poster-preview.png") render_poster(p, "poster.pdf")                            # true A1 size render_poster(p, "preview.png", scale = 0.25, dpi = 150)   # A4-ish preview

what: Figure card content: a ggplot styled for poster font size — card_figure
title: Figure card content: a ggplot styled for poster font size — card_figure
text: Applies poster's base font size/family ggplot's theme, converts grob.

title: Figure card content: a ggplot styled for poster font size — card_figure
code: card_figure(gg, theme = poster_theme(), width = NULL, height = NULL)

title: Figure card content: a ggplot styled for poster font size — card_figure
text: gg ggplot object. theme poster_theme() object. width, height Target size grid::unit millimetres. NULL keeps plot's natural size.

title: Figure card content: a ggplot styled for poster font size — card_figure
text: grob suitable body argument poster_card().

title: Figure card content: a ggplot styled for poster font size — card_figure
code: g <- card_figure(ggplot2::qplot(1, 1), poster_theme()) #> Warning: `qplot()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.

what: Image card content: one or more photos arranged in a row, with optional labels — card_image
title: Image card content: one or more photos arranged in a row, with optional labels — card_image
text: Reads image files magick::image_read(), converts raster grobs, lays horizontal strip – e.g. "dominant species" photo band, sample poster.

title: Image card content: one or more photos arranged in a row, with optional labels — card_image
code: card_image(   files,   labels = NULL,   theme = poster_theme(),   height = NULL,   width = NULL,   space = 3,   label_position = c("below", "inside") )

title: Image card content: one or more photos arranged in a row, with optional labels — card_image
text: files Character vector image file paths. labels Optional character vector captions, length files. theme poster_theme() object. height Height photo, grid::unit millimetres. NULL (default, width also NULL) uses 60mm. width Alternative height: target total row width (photos plus gaps ). photo's height solved , aspect ratio, row sums width – useful making photo band fill specific fraction card's width regardless many photos . Takes precedence height given. space Gap photos, grid::unit millimetres. label_position "" (default) draws caption row photo. "inside" overlays near bottom photo (white text semi-transparent bar), row's height just photo height.

title: Image card content: one or more photos arranged in a row, with optional labels — card_image
text: grob suitable body argument poster_card().

title: Image card content: one or more photos arranged in a row, with optional labels — card_image
code: f <- system.file("extdata", "small.JPG", package = "ggposter") g <- card_image(f, labels = "sample", theme = poster_theme())

what: Table card content: a booktab-style table grob — card_table
title: Table card content: a booktab-style table grob — card_table
text: Builds table title/caption simple top/bottom/header rules (booktab style), based gridExtra::tableGrob().

title: Table card content: a booktab-style table grob — card_table
code: card_table(   x,   theme = poster_theme(),   title = NULL,   caption = NULL,   rows = NULL,   width = NULL )

title: Table card content: a booktab-style table grob — card_table
text: x data.frame tibble. theme poster_theme() object. title Optional title text drawn table. caption Optional caption text drawn table. rows Passed gridExtra::tableGrob(); NULL (default) hides row names. width Target width, grid::unit millimetres. table naturally wider, font size shrunk fit (final clip safety net). NULL leaves table natural size.

title: Table card content: a booktab-style table grob — card_table
text: grob suitable body argument poster_card().

title: Table card content: a booktab-style table grob — card_table
code: g <- card_table(head(iris), poster_theme(), title = "Iris")

what: Text card content: a bullet list rendered as markdown — card_text
title: Text card content: a bullet list rendered as markdown — card_text
text: Wraps character vector markdown lines body grob using gridtext::textbox_grob(), bold/italic/links work. line laid row, one per element md; "- "-prefixed line gets bullet narrow column wrapped continuation line aligns item's text rather bullet (hanging indent) – gridtext CSS text-indent/padding-left support within single call, item measured wrapped separately instead.

title: Text card content: a bullet list rendered as markdown — card_text
code: card_text(md, theme = poster_theme(), width = NULL)

title: Text card content: a bullet list rendered as markdown — card_text
text: md Character vector. element becomes one paragraph/bullet. Markdown syntax (e.g. **bold**) honoured. theme poster_theme() object. width Wrap width, grid::unit millimetres. NULL lets grob size natural width (wrapping).

title: Text card content: a bullet list rendered as markdown — card_text
text: grob suitable body argument poster_card().

title: Text card content: a bullet list rendered as markdown — card_text
code: g <- card_text(c("- point one", "- point two"), poster_theme())

what: ggposter: build A1 conference posters from ggplot2 — ggposter-package
title: ggposter: build A1 conference posters from ggplot2 — ggposter-package
text: ggposter declares poster title band plus columns rounded, tab-headed cards (text, tables, figures, photo strips), assembled grid gtable::gtable rendered true size embedded fonts. See poster() build one R list YAML file, package vignette (vignette("ggposter")) full walkthrough.

title: ggposter: build A1 conference posters from ggplot2 — ggposter-package
text: Maintainer: Toshikazu Matsumura matutosi@gmail.com

what: Build a poster from a declarative spec — poster
title: Build a poster from a declarative spec — poster
text: Assembles title band multi-column body section cards single gtable::gtable sized real paper size (default A1 portrait). spec can R list (built hand, reading YAML read_poster_yaml()) path YAML file, case read automatically.

title: Build a poster from a declarative spec — poster
code: poster(spec, objects = list(), theme = NULL, base_dir = NULL)

title: Build a poster from a declarative spec — poster
text: spec R list following schema , path YAML file. objects Named list R objects (ggplots, data.frames, grobs) referenced sections[[.]]$body$object spec. theme poster_theme() object. NULL (default), built spec$theme. base_dir Directory used resolve relative image paths spec. Defaults YAML file's directory, working directory list spec.

title: Build a poster from a declarative spec — poster
text: object class ggposter, thin wrapper around gtable::gtable. Print preview, pass render_poster() save PDF/PNG true size.

title: Build a poster from a declarative spec — poster
text: See package vignette full spec schema. short: poster size (e.g. "A1") orientation. theme Arguments passed poster_theme(). title Arguments passed poster_title(). layout columns, left, right: section names go column, top bottom. sections Named list; header, optional relative height, body type "text", "table", "figure", "image" plus type's arguments.

title: Build a poster from a declarative spec — poster
code: spec <- list(   title = list(title = "Example poster"),   layout = list(left = "intro", right = "outro"),   sections = list(     intro = list(header = "INTRO", body = list(type = "text", md = "- Hello")),     outro = list(header = "OUTRO", body = list(type = "text", md = "- Bye"))   ) ) p <- poster(spec)

what: Build a section card: a rounded box with a header tab and content — poster_card
title: Build a section card: a rounded box with a header tab and content — poster_card
text: card repeating unit poster. draws rounded background box (border theme accent), optional header "tab" accent colour, body grob underneath. Every content type (text, table, figure, image) wrapped card poster keeps one consistent look.

title: Build a section card: a rounded box with a header tab and content — poster_card
code: poster_card(   body,   header = NULL,   theme = poster_theme(),   header_size = NULL,   fit_content = FALSE )

title: Build a section card: a rounded box with a header tab and content — poster_card
text: body content. Either grob, ggplot object (converted ggplot2::ggplotGrob()). header Header label tab. NULL, NA, "" draws header. theme poster_theme() object. header_size Font size header, points. Defaults theme$base_size * 1.15. fit_content TRUE, size body cell body's measured height times theme$content_pad_factor, instead filling however much vertical space card given (latter want card's height relative share column, e.g. poster()'s numeric section$height; former section$height = "auto" uses).

title: Build a section card: a rounded box with a header tab and content — poster_card
text: gtable::gtable grob class poster_card.

title: Build a section card: a rounded box with a header tab and content — poster_card
text: returned object gtable::gtable (grob), can placed another gtable drawn directly grid::grid.draw().

title: Build a section card: a rounded box with a header tab and content — poster_card
code: body <- grid::textGrob("hello") card <- poster_card(body, header = "OBJECTIVES", theme = poster_theme()) # grid::grid.newpage(); grid::grid.draw(card)

what: Find an available font family for a poster — poster_font
title: Find an available font family for a poster — poster_font
text: Chooses font family actually installed system, output renders (embeds PDF) without falling back substitute. Latin text prefers clean sans-serif faces; CJK text prefers Japanese capable family.

title: Find an available font family for a poster — poster_font
code: poster_font(cjk = FALSE, preferred = NULL, fallback = "")

title: Find an available font family for a poster — poster_font
text: cjk TRUE, look CJK (Japanese) capable family. preferred Character vector family names try, order. NULL, built-list common families used. fallback Family name return none preferred installed. Defaults empty string, lets graphics device pick.

title: Find an available font family for a poster — poster_font
text: single font family name (character scalar).

title: Find an available font family for a poster — poster_font
code: poster_font() #> [1] "DejaVu Sans" poster_font(cjk = TRUE) #> [1] ""

what: Register a font so its exact file is used by graphics devices — poster_register_font
title: Register a font so its exact file is used by graphics devices — poster_register_font
text: Thin convenience wrapper systemfonts::register_font(); useful specific TTF/OTF must embedded (e.g. lab-mandated typeface).

title: Register a font so its exact file is used by graphics devices — poster_register_font
code: poster_register_font(   family,   plain,   bold = plain,   italic = plain,   bolditalic = plain )

title: Register a font so its exact file is used by graphics devices — poster_register_font
text: family Name register font . plain, bold, italic, bolditalic Paths font files face. plain required.

title: Register a font so its exact file is used by graphics devices — poster_register_font
text: Invisibly, registered family name.

what: Theme (visual settings) for a ggposter poster — poster_theme
title: Theme (visual settings) for a ggposter poster — poster_theme
text: poster_theme() bundles colours, fonts, spacing used across poster. Every card, title band, content builder receives theme object whole poster shares one look. Individual values can overridden per call.

title: Theme (visual settings) for a ggposter poster — poster_theme
code: poster_theme(   accent = "#2E7D32",   header_text = "#FFFFFF",   box_fill = "#FFFFFF",   box_border = NULL,   body_text = "#1A1A1A",   corner_radius = 5,   border_width = 2,   base_size = 26,   base_family = NULL,   cjk_family = NULL,   pad = NULL,   gap = 6,   lineheight = 0.95,   content_pad_factor = 1.2 )

title: Theme (visual settings) for a ggposter poster — poster_theme
text: accent Accent colour section-header tabs box borders. header_text Text colour used accent-coloured header tab. box_fill Fill colour section box (card background). box_border Border colour section box. Defaults accent. body_text Colour body text. corner_radius Corner radius rounded boxes, grid::unit number interpreted millimetres. border_width Line width box borders (points). base_size Base font size points (A1 output). base_family Font family Latin text. NULL picks sensible default via poster_font(). cjk_family Font family CJK (Japanese) text. NULL falls back base_family. pad Inner padding card, grid::unit millimetres. NULL (default) uses one line's height base_size/lineheight – physical-unit conversion font size, text measurement, scales base_size without needing real output device open. gap Gap stacked cards, grid::unit millimetres. lineheight Line spacing multiplier wrapped text (title band card_text()), passed grid::gpar(). Values 1 (e.g. 0.95) tuck wrapped lines closer together text-rendering defaults. content_pad_factor Multiplier applied block text/table content's measured height get enclosing box's height, used wherever poster element sizes fit content (title band, card height = "auto"; see poster()). 1.2 means 20% breathing room /content.

title: Theme (visual settings) for a ggposter poster — poster_theme
text: object class poster_theme.

title: Theme (visual settings) for a ggposter poster — poster_theme
code: th <- poster_theme(accent = "#2E7D32") th$accent #> [1] "#2E7D32"

what: Build the full-width title band of a poster — poster_title
title: Build the full-width title band of a poster — poster_title
text: Build full-width title band poster

title: Build the full-width title band of a poster — poster_title
code: poster_title(   title,   subtitle = NULL,   authors = NULL,   affiliations = NULL,   funding = NULL,   logo = NULL,   theme = poster_theme(),   width = NULL )

title: Build the full-width title band of a poster — poster_title
text: title Main title string. subtitle Optional subtitle string. authors Optional author list string (e.g. "*. One, B. Two"). affiliations Optional affiliations string. funding Optional acknowledgement/funding string, drawn smaller. logo Optional path logo image, placed right edge. theme poster_theme() object. width Wrap width text rows, grid::unit millimetres. Typically poster width minus outer margins. NULL disables wrapping (recommended long titles).

title: Build the full-width title band of a poster — poster_title
text: grob spanning full poster width.

title: Build the full-width title band of a poster — poster_title
code: g <- poster_title("A Title", authors = "*A. Author", theme = poster_theme())

title: Read a poster spec from a YAML file — read_poster_yaml
code: path <- system.file("extdata", "poster_sample.yml", package = "ggposter") spec <- read_poster_yaml(path)

what: Render a poster to PDF or PNG at true size — render_poster
title: Render a poster to PDF or PNG at true size — render_poster
text: Saves poster() object real paper size (e.g. 594x841mm A1). output format chosen file's extension: .pdf uses grDevices::cairo_pdf (vector output CJK glyph embedding), anything else uses ragg::agg_png.

title: Render a poster to PDF or PNG at true size — render_poster
code: render_poster(x, file, scale = 1, dpi = 300)

title: Render a poster to PDF or PNG at true size — render_poster
text: x ggposter object, returned poster(). file Output file path. Extension determines device (.pdf .png). scale Shrink factor applied true paper size, e.g. 0.25 A4-sized preview A1 poster. Font/line sizes stay proportional whole canvas (just DPI) scaled. dpi Resolution PNG output. Ignored PDF.

title: Render a poster to PDF or PNG at true size — render_poster
text: output file path, invisibly.

title: Render a poster to PDF or PNG at true size — render_poster
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ p <- poster(spec) render_poster(p, "poster.pdf") render_poster(p, "preview.png", scale = 0.25) } # }
